data.title: Gala Technology Sets 2026 AGM to Renew Governance Mandates and Expand Share Issuance Authority
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Gala Technology Holding Limited ( (HK:2458) ) has shared an announcement.

Gala Technology Holding Limited has called its annual general meeting for 25 June 2026 in Shenzhen, where shareholders will vote on adopting the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and reappointing Grant Thornton Hong Kong as independent auditor. The meeting will also consider the re-election of two independent non-executive directors and a mandate allowing the board to issue up to 20% of the company’s share capital, a step that could provide additional financial flexibility and support future capital-raising or strategic initiatives.

These resolutions, if approved, would reaffirm the company’s existing governance arrangements while equipping directors with broader authority over equity issuance within prescribed limits. The share issuance mandate, excluding treasury shares and specific exempted scenarios such as rights issues and share option schemes, may influence future dilution dynamics and capital structure decisions, and signals management’s intent to retain optionality for funding growth or other corporate actions.

By renewing the auditor’s appointment and empowering the board on director remuneration, Gala Technology underscores continuity in its oversight and reporting framework. The AGM outcomes will be closely watched by investors for any indication of shifts in corporate strategy, governance priorities, or balance sheet management that could affect shareholder value.

More about Gala Technology Holding Limited

Gala Technology Holding Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating within the technology sector and subject to the regulatory framework of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. The company maintains an independent board structure with non-executive directors and engages external auditors to review its consolidated financial statements for shareholders.

Average Trading Volume: 41,451

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$707.8M

data.title: Black Sesame International Sets June EGM, Closes Register for Acquisition Vote
data.description:

  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Black Sesame International Holding Limited ( (HK:2533) ) has shared an update.

Black Sesame International Holding Limited has called an extraordinary general meeting for June 17, 2026, to seek shareholder approval on matters related to a previously announced acquisition. The company will close its register of members from June 12 to June 17, 2026, meaning only shareholders on record as of June 17, or those who register share transfers by 4:30 p.m. on June 11, will be eligible to attend and vote.

The company will issue a circular in due course setting out the proposed resolution and formal notice of the meeting on both the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and its own website. The move underscores the company’s need for formal shareholder consent on the acquisition, highlighting standard governance procedures that may influence ownership positions and voting dynamics among existing investors.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2533) stock is a Sell with a HK$16.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Black Sesame International Holding Limited stock, see the HK:2533 Stock Forecast page.

More about Black Sesame International Holding Limited

Black Sesame International Holding Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 2533. The company is governed by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, and it is engaged in corporate activities that include acquisitions requiring shareholder approval.

The company also confirmed it holds no treasury or repurchased shares pending cancellation, and approved the payment of a final dividend, underscoring stable corporate governance and a continued capital return to investors.

Zhejiang Expressway Co ( (HK:0576) ) has provided an update.

Zhejiang Expressway Co., Ltd. announced that the Shanghai Stock Exchange M&A and Restructuring Review Committee has approved its proposed absorption and merger of Oceanking Development through a share swap, along with an issuance of A shares under a specific mandate. The committee concluded that the transaction meets reorganisation conditions and information disclosure requirements, marking a key regulatory milestone for the deal.

The company cautioned that the transaction still depends on the satisfaction of conditions precedent set out in the merger and share swap agreements, meaning it may not proceed, become unconditional, or take effect. Zhejiang Expressway said it will issue further announcements as the transaction progresses and advised investors to exercise caution and not rely solely on its published information when dealing in its securities.

data.title: Cirtek Moves to Replace PwC Amid Audit Dispute and Results Delay
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

The latest announcement is out from Cirtek Holdings Limited ( (HK:1433) ).

Cirtek Holdings plans to remove PricewaterhouseCoopers as its auditor and appoint Grant Thornton Hong Kong, subject to shareholder approval at an extraordinary general meeting, after a dispute over the handling of an investigation into certain payments by an overseas subsidiary. The disagreement, centered on audit arrangements and the composition of the Bangladesh component audit team, has contributed to a delay in publishing the 2025 annual results and a continued suspension of trading in the company’s shares, underscoring governance and transparency concerns for investors and regulators.

PwC had raised red flags over payments recorded under property, plant and equipment and other expenses, recommending an independent investigation by reputable professional investigators and legal counsel. The firm stated it had not received sufficient information about the proposed investigators, legal advisers, or the scope of the probe to assess their competence and independence, and it maintained that the existing component audit team, which identified the issues, should remain in place, highlighting a serious breakdown in alignment between the auditor and the company’s management and audit committee.

More about Cirtek Holdings Limited

Cirtek Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated investment holding company listed in Hong Kong, with operations that include overseas subsidiaries such as in Bangladesh. The group’s activities involve owning and managing property, plant and equipment and incurring related operating expenses across its international footprint, exposing it to multi-jurisdictional audit and compliance requirements.

Average Trading Volume: 1,424,285

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: HK$162M

Dekon Food and Agriculture Group announced that its controlling shareholder, chairman and executive director, Wang Degen, has increased his stake in the company by purchasing 648,300 H shares on the open market between 27 and 29 May 2026. The transaction, executed at an average price of about HK$52.07 per share for a total consideration of roughly HK$33.76 million, raised his interest in the company’s issued share capital by approximately 0.1667%.

The move is presented by Wang as a show of confidence in the group’s long-term development and growth prospects, signaling management’s positive view of the company’s future performance and potentially supporting market sentiment toward the stock. The board also reminded shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution when dealing in the company’s shares, underscoring the inherent risks associated with equity investment despite the insider share purchase.

data.title: CTR Holdings Tightens Nomination Committee Rules to Bolster Governance
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

CTR Holdings Limited ( (HK:1416) ) has issued an update.

CTR Holdings Limited has updated the terms of reference for its Nomination Committee, originally established in 2019, to ensure continued compliance with the Hong Kong Stock Exchange’s Listing Rules and to formalize its governance framework around board appointments. The revised terms specify that the committee must comprise at least three directors with a majority being independent non-executive directors, include at least one member of a different gender, and clarify procedures for appointments, removals, meeting notices, and tenure, underscoring the company’s focus on board diversity, transparency, and regulatory alignment.

The updated mandate also sets out clear rules for convening meetings, including notice periods and acceptable communication methods, which is expected to streamline committee operations and enhance accountability in nomination processes. These changes reinforce CTR Holdings’ corporate governance structure, potentially improving investor confidence by demonstrating a structured approach to board oversight and diversity as regulatory expectations in Hong Kong continue to evolve.

More about CTR Holdings Limited

CTR Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, operating under the governance framework required for Hong Kong-listed issuers. Its board structure includes committees such as the Nomination Committee, which oversees director appointments and board composition to align with regulatory standards and corporate governance best practices.

Ever Reach Group Holdings Co., Ltd. ( (HK:3616) ) has provided an update.

Ever Reach Group has agreed to sell its entire 70% equity stake in Xuchang Yuanda Real Estate Company Limited, an indirect non-wholly owned subsidiary, to Xuchang Fengzhida Real Estate Company Limited for RMB3 million. The disposal means Ever Reach will cease to hold any interest in the target, which will no longer be consolidated into the group’s financial statements, slightly reshaping its asset base and project exposure.

The transaction is classified as both a discloseable and connected transaction under Hong Kong listing rules because the purchaser is a substantial shareholder of the target and therefore a connected person at the subsidiary level. With board approval and confirmation by independent non-executive directors that the terms are fair, reasonable and on normal commercial terms, the deal requires only public announcement, streamlining execution while signaling governance safeguards for minority shareholders.

More about Ever Reach Group Holdings Co., Ltd.

Ever Reach Group (Holdings) Company Limited is a Cayman Islands–incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating through subsidiaries in the mainland China property sector. Its business involves real estate development and related property interests, with holdings in various project companies that it consolidates into its group financial statements.

Hong Kong Finance Group Ltd. ( (HK:1273) ) has provided an update.

Hong Kong Finance Group has agreed to extend a new secured loan of HK$4 million to three repeat customers under a nine-month facility carrying an interest rate of 18% per annum. United Co RUSAL ( (HK:0486) ) has provided an announcement.

United Company RUSAL has disclosed that it paid coupon income on its 001PC-05 series non-documentary commercial bonds maturing in September 2027, issued under its 001РС commercial bonds programme. The bonds, with a nominal value of 1,000 rubles each and a total of 30 million outstanding, carry ISIN RU000A10B065.

For the fifteenth coupon period running from April 28 to May 28, 2026, the company paid a total of 374.4 million rubles in interest to bondholders. The coupon amounted to an annualized rate of 15.18%, equivalent to 12.48 rubles per bond, and was settled in non-cash Russian ruble payments on May 28, 2026, underscoring RUSAL’s ongoing servicing of its domestic debt obligations.

The company is listed in Hong Kong, Moscow and St. Petersburg, providing investors with exposure to the global aluminium market and related financial instruments.

data.title: Shanghai INT Medical Sets Nomination Committee Rules to Strengthen Board Governance
data.description:

The latest announcement is out from Shanghai Kindly Medical Instruments Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1501) ).

Shanghai INT Medical Instruments has formally established a Nomination Committee under its board, defining its composition, authority and working procedures in line with PRC Company Law, the Hong Kong Listing Rules and the company’s Articles of Association. The committee will be mainly composed of independent non-executive directors, include at least one director of a different gender, and be supported by the company secretary.

The Nomination Committee is mandated to oversee board structure, diversity and succession planning, including maintaining a board skills matrix and recommending appointments and reappointments of directors and senior management. PATEO CONNECT Technology (Shanghai) Corporation Class H ( (HK:2889) ) has issued an announcement.

PATEO CONNECT Technology (Shanghai) Corporation, a Hong Kong-listed provider of intelligent vehicle connectivity and automotive software solutions, is expanding its focus on advanced in-vehicle AI and autonomous driving technologies to support global automakers and mobility partners. The Group aims to strengthen its position in next-generation smart cockpit and connected car platforms as the automotive industry accelerates digital transformation.

The company announced it has held a strategic cooperation signing ceremony with NVIDIA in the United States, where both sides discussed potential collaboration in in-vehicle AI, autonomous driving, next-generation computing platforms, and optical communication. While the event reflects PATEO CONNECT’s intent to deepen ties with a leading global chip and AI platform provider, the parties have not yet formed binding obligations, and any concrete cooperation arrangements will be disclosed later in accordance with regulatory requirements, leaving investors to watch for future updates on how the partnership may reshape the Group’s technology roadmap and competitive standing.
